title: Eulophia angornensis
intro: Eulophia angornensis, also known as the Angora orchid or Eulophia, is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the potential to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eulophia angornensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Eulophia angornensis is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Eulophia angornensis is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. This species belongs to the subtribe Coryciinae, which includes various terrestrial orchids. Eulophia angornensis is further classified under the genus Eulophia, a group of orchids native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Eulophia angornensis is also based on its unique characteristics, such as its growth habit and flower morphology. Further study and research are necessary to confirm the exact classification of this species.
